xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfRegisteredOfficeOfCom: EL-6, TTC Industrial Area, Electronics Zone, MIDC Mahape, Navi Mumbai 400710
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfCorporateOfficeOfComp: EL-6, TTC Industrial Area, Electronics Zone, MIDC Mahape, Navi Mumbai 400710
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescriptionOfBusinessActivity: Wired, wireless or satellite Telecommunication services
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProductOrServiceSoldByTheEntit: Other wired, wireless, and satellite telecommunication activities
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ABriefOnTypesOfCustomersExplan: Nelco provides satellite communication services for multiple segments and use-cases in Enterprise and Government such as ATM, Bank branches, Education, Offshore oil exploaration, renewable energy, maritime communication and inflight Wi-Fi services.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Robust customer relationship management can help the Company to strengthen its market leadership position in key segments, build competitive advantages and enhance growth.

Nelco relies on its supply chain partners for Satcom equipment and bandwidth capacity. Any disruptions in timely supply, price escalations, or regulatory compliance issues may impact the Company’s operations and its ability to deliver satisfactory services to customers. Effective Supply Chain Management is also critical to build competitive advantages.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM:
Diversification of Sources Engage multiple suppliers for critical components and services to reduce dependency on a single vendor.
Supplier Code of Conduct & ESG Integration Mandate adherence to Nelco’s Supplier Code of Conduct, covering ethics, labor practices, and environmental standards; integrate ESG criteria into vendor agreements and purchase orders.
Digital Tools for Visibility Deploy technology platforms for inventory tracking.
Business Continuity Planning Maintain contingency plans for critical supplies, including alternate sourcing arrangements and buffer stock strategies.
Compliance & Audit Mechanisms Periodic audits and compliance checks to ensure alignment with regulatory and internal standards.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: The Company on boards and builds newer competencies to aid its multifold growth. The Company also plans to enhance skills of existing people and build future required competencies.

Effective corporate governance and strong ethical standards are essential to maintain stakeholder trust, mitigate risks and ensure compliance. Prioritizing these areas supports sustainable business practices and long-term success.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM:
Strong Board Oversight An independent and diverse Board supported by specialized committees (Audit, Risk Management, NRC) to oversee governance and ethical practices.
Regulatory Compliance Framework Implemented Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and periodic compliance audits to ensure adherence to SEBI, MCA, and other statutory requirements.
Digital Monitoring and Reporting Use of technology platforms for compliance tracking and automated alerts for timely update.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: The Company’s infrastructure is vulnerable to interruptions from natural disasters, power outages, IT systems failures, security breaches, and human errors, such events can significantly impact service quality and customer satisfaction. A robust business continuity framework and integrated risk management is key to enhance Company’s resilience and long-term business success.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM:
Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) Framework Business continuity risks integrated into the ERM system, with periodic reviews by the Risk Management Committee and Board oversight.
Business Continuity Planning (BCP) Maintaining and regularly updating BCPs for critical processes, including disaster recovery protocols.
Technology and Infrastructure Resilience Implement redundant systems, data backup and DR for business-critical data to minimize downtime during emergencies.
Training and Awareness Conduct periodic drills and employee training on emergency response and risk mitigation strategies.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Operating in the satellite communication industry, Nelco prioritizes cybersecurity and data protection to prevent data breaches and cyber attacks. Such incidents can expose the organization to significant vulnerabilities, leading to financial and reputational loss. Ensuring robust cybersecurity measures is vital for maintaining trust, avoiding financial loss and ensuring operational integrity.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM:
Robust Information Security Framework ISO/IEC 27001: 2022 certification ensuring a robust Information Security Management system.
Regular Vulnerability Assessments & Penetration Testing (VAPT) The company conducts periodic security audits, penetration tests, and vulnerability scans to identify and remediate risks proactively.
Advanced Threat Detection & Monitoring Nelco deploys real-time monitoring tools, with automated alerts for suspicious activities.
Data Privacy Compliance We align with applicable data protection laws and internal privacy policies; ensure secure handling of customer and employee data.
Access Control & Encryption Enforce strict role-based access controls for sensitive data.
Employee Awareness & Training The company conducts cybersecurity and data privacy awareness & training for all employees, including phishing simulation exercises on a regular basis.
Third-Party Risk Management Assess cybersecurity posture of vendors and partners; include security clauses in contracts and monitor compliance.
Continuous Improvement Leverage threat intelligence, industry benchmarks, and lessons learned from incidents to strengthen security posture.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Digitalization enables Nelco to enhance operational efficiency, improve customer experience, and strengthen competitive advantage in the rapidly evolving telecom and satellite communications sector. By leveraging advanced technologies such as automation, AI ML analytics, and cloud platforms, the company can optimize processes, reduce costs, and accelerate service delivery. Digital tools also provide real-time insights for better decision-making, improve cybersecurity resilience, and support scalable solutions for emerging markets. This transformation aligns with Nelco’s strategic vision of innovation-driven growth and positions the company to capture new business opportunities while meeting sustainability and governance objectives.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: People Well being, Health & Safety, Diversity equity & Inclusion
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DE&I) are crucial for the company to promote fair treatment and full participation of all employees, including individuals of different races, ethnicities, religions, abilities, genders, and sexual orientations. A diverse, equitable, and inclusive ecosystem enables Nelco to effectively respond to challenges, retain top talent, and better meet the needs of various stakeholders. Additionally, prioritizing people’s wellbeing, health, and safety fosters a productive and hazard-free workplace, contributing positively to the Company’s growth and development.

Emission reduction and energy management help Nelco to enhance operational efficiency and optimize energy use, thereby reducing operational costs. By focusing on these areas, Nelco can minimize its environmental footprint, comply with regulatory requirements, and contribute to global sustainability efforts. This strategic approach not only supports cost savings but also strengthens the Company’s reputation as a responsible and forward thinking organization.

Community development offers Nelco a strategic avenue to build trust and create long-term value for stakeholders. By investing in education, digital literacy, and skill development programs, the company helps empower local communities, enhance employability, and bridge the digital divide. These initiatives not only align with national priorities and sustainability goals but also foster goodwill, improve brand reputation, and support inclusive growth. Engaging with communities proactively helps Nelco mitigate social risks, attract talent, and unlock new market opportunities.

Efficient waste and water management help the company lower operational costs, enhance its reputation and demonstrate its commitment to environmental sustainability. This strategic focus not only supports regulatory compliance but also strengthens the Company’s image as a responsible and eco- friendly organization.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheNationalAndInternatio: The company is audited and certified as per international standards like TL 9000, ISO 27001, ISO 20000. The policies and their implementation are evaluated as part of these audits. Additionally, the policies meet the regulatory requirements such as SEBI Listing Regulations, labor laws, Cybersecurity and data privacy requirements etc. The policies reflect Tata groups commitment to improve the quality of life of the communities it serves and practice of giving back to the society.

The company is audited and certified as per international standards like TL 9000, ISO 27001, ISO 20000. The policies and their implementation are evaluated as part of these audits. Additionally, the policies meet the regulatory requirements such as SEBI Listing Regulations, labor laws, Cybersecurity and data privacy requirements etc. The policies reflect Tata groups commitment to improve the quality of life of the communities it serves and practice of giving back to the society.

Additionally, the policies meet the regulatory requirements such as SEBI Listing Regulations, labor laws, Cybersecurity and data privacy requirements etc. The policies reflect Tata groups commitment to improve the quality of life of the communities it serves and practice of giving back to the society.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:SpecificCommitmentsGoalsAndTar: Yes, Nelco has developed an ESG strategy for their journey in environmental, social, and governance (ESG) practices. An ESG strategy is defined with sustainability goals.
Yes, Nelco has developed an ESG strategy for their journey in environmental, social, and governance (ESG) practices. An ESG strategy is defined with sustainability goals.
Yes, Nelco has developed an ESG strategy for their journey in environmental, social, and governance (ESG) practices. An ESG strategy is defined with sustainability goals.

Yes, Nelco has developed an ESG strategy for their journey in environmental, social, and governance (ESG) practices. An ESG strategy is defined with sustainability goals.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PerformanceOfTheEntityAgainstT: Nelco is in the process of integrating its key material priorities into its business as part of the overall ESG strategy. Three-year goals are established, and the company is committed towards achieving them.

Nelco is in the process of integrating its key material priorities into its business as part of the overall ESG strategy. Three-year goals are established, and the company is committed towards achieving them.

Nelco is in the process of integrating its key material priorities into its business as part of the overall ESG strategy. Three-year goals are established, and the company is committed towards achieving them.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StatementByDirectorResponsible: As a globally trusted Satellite communication service provider, the Company’s ESG vision is to generate enduring value for all stakeholders by embedding environmental sustainability, social inclusivity, and governance excellence across its operations. Through ethical business practices, continuous improvement, and commitment to social equity, Nelco is building a resilient, responsible, and future-ready enterprise. We reaffirm our commitment to strong ESG governance as the bedrock of sustainable growth. Our oversight mechanisms have been further strengthened to ensure transparency and accountability across all operations. A key focus area this year is the comprehensive monitoring of Scope 1, Scope 2, and Scope 3 emissions, enabling us to set focused targets and rigorously track progress towards carbon reduction goals. With the aim of embedding sustainability into our supply chain management, we are working closely with partners to enhance resilience, ethical practices, and environmental performance. At the same time, we continue to prioritize our people – investing in skill development, fostering diversity and inclusion, and cultivating a safe, equitable workplace. Through these initiatives, Nelco aims not only to meet regulatory expectations but to lead by example in building a responsible, future-ready organization. Together, we advance innovation, strengthen stakeholder trust, and contribute meaningfully to ESG objectives. Together, we turn ESG commitment into lasting value for our people, partners, and the planet – because at Nelco, doing business responsibly is the only way we know.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheHighestAuthorityRe: Name: PJ Nath (DIN: 05118177) Designation: Managing Director and CEO
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSpecifiedCommitteeOfT: Name: PJ Nath (DIN: 05118177) Designation: Managing Director and CEO
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AntiCorruptionOrAntiBriberyPol: Yes. Nelco maintains a strong commitment to preventing bribery and unethical business practices across all operations. As part of the Tata Group, the Company adheres to the Tata Code of Conduct (TCoC), which sets out comprehensive ethical standards for employees, business partners, and third parties associated with Nelco. In addition, Nelco has an independent Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption (ABAC) Policy that complements the TCoC and provides detailed guidelines to reinforce integrity in business dealings. This policy is reviewed periodically to ensure continued relevance and compliance. The ABAC Policy is publicly available on the Company’s website at https://www.nelco.in/pdf/Policies/ABAC%20Policy.pdf
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheEntityHaveProcesse:
Yes, Nelco has established robust processes to avoid and manage conflicts of interest involving members of the Board. Key measures include: i. Distinct Roles: The role of the Chairman of the Board is non-executive and distinct from that of the Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer (MD & CEO). This separation ensures a clear distinction between governance and management functions. ii. Formal Policies for Conflict of Interest and Related Party Transactions: Nelco maintains a comprehensive Conflict of Interest Policy applicable to all employees, including Board members and KMPs. The policy defines conflicts as any personal, financial, or relational interest (including those of immediate family) that could compromise objective decision making or the Company’s interests. iii. Mandatory Disclosure Requirements: Board members, executives, and employees are required to disclose actual, potential, or perceived conflicts to the HR Head or Company Secretary. These disclosures are reviewed promptly to determine appropriate action. iv. Prevention of Undue Influence: Identified conflicts trigger following safeguards: a. Individuals involved refrain from participating in relevant discussions or decisions. b. In case of familial relationships with vendors, suppliers, or other business associates, the affected member is recused from related deliberations. v. Periodic Review and Oversight: Disclosures are reviewed annually, and updates are collected as situations evolve. The HR Head and Company Secretary oversee compliance through documented logs and report any new conflicts to the Board. The updated Policies may be accessed at the following web link: https://www.nelco.in/pdf/Policies/conflict-of-interest-policy.pdf?csrt=1826613678760159899 https://www.nelco.in/pdf/Policies/Related%20Party%20Transaction%20Policy.pdf?csrt=18266136787601598 99 vi. Tata Code of Conduct (TCoC): The TCoC includes clauses prohibiting any employee, including the Managing Director (MD), from accepting any position of responsibility, with or without compensation, in any other organization without prior written approval from the Company. For the MD, such approval must be obtained from the Board.
These processes are integral to maintaining transparency, accountability, and trust within the organization.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TopicsOrPrinciplesCoveredUnder: POSH (Prevention of Sexual Harassment), Ethics, Safety & Health, Training on TCoC
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesInPlaceToS: The Company actively minimizes plastic usage by reducing packaging materials and promoting recyclable or biodegradable alternatives. Initiatives include replacing plastic wraps with reusable lashing belts for material storage and discouraging the use of packaged water in disposable plastic bottles.

Nelco operates its own product repair center to extend product life and reduce waste. When remote sites are decommissioned, electronic hardware and antennas are relocated to other sites for reuse. Electronic scrap is responsibly disposed of through certified e-waste management service providers approved by the Central and State Pollution Control Boards.

Hazardous waste is managed in strict compliance with applicable regulations. Specialized processes ensure safe handling, storage, and disposal through certified hazardous waste management service providers to prevent environmental contamination and safeguard stakeholder health.

The Company follows a holistic approach to waste management, emphasizing reduction, reuse, and recycling. Measures include waste segregation, use of eco-friendly materials, and collaboration with certified waste management partners for responsible disposal of non-recyclable waste. Additionally, employees are encouraged to avoid unnecessary paper printouts unless essential, supporting resource conservation.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfEqualOppertunityPolic:
Nelco adheres to the Tata Code of Conduct (TCoC), which explicitly incorporates the principles of equal opportunity and non-discrimination, including provisions aligned with the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016. The Company is committed to creating an inclusive workplace where individuals are treated fairly and provided equal opportunities regardless of disability or any other characteristic.
The detailed Equal Opportunity Policy is available on our website at: https://www.nelco.in/investor-relation/ corporate-governance.php,
https://www.nelco.in/pdf/TCoC_2017.pdf
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMechanismAvailableToR:
Nelco has robust mechanisms to receive and redress grievances for employees and workers across multiple categories: i. Whistle Blower & Vigil Mechanism (WBVM): The Company has adopted a WBVM Policy that enables employees to report concerns related to unethical behavior, suspected fraud, or violations of the Company’s Code of Conduct. Complaints can be escalated to the Chief Ethics Counsellor or the Chairman of the Audit Committee for impartial review and resolution. Employees may also raise any ethical concerns through ethics@nelco.in or report issues to identified ethics committee members.
ii. Prevention of Sexual Harassment (POSH): Nelco has implemented a POSH Policy in compliance with statutory requirements. An Internal Committee (IC) is constituted to investigate and address complaints of sexual harassment, discrimination, or hostile behavior, ensuring confidentiality and fair proceedings.
iii. Health & Safety Grievance Portal: Employees and stakeholders have access to a dedicated portal to report health and safety-related grievances or observations. These submissions are reviewed by the Apex Safety Committee, and corrective actions are implemented promptly. Feedback on resolution is communicated to the complainant to ensure transparency.
These mechanisms collectively reinforce Nelco’s commitment to ethical conduct, workplace safety, and employee well-being.

Nelco has robust mechanisms to receive and redress grievances for employees and workers across multiple categories: i. Whistle Blower & Vigil Mechanism (WBVM): The Company has adopted a WBVM Policy that enables employees to report concerns related to unethical behavior, suspected fraud, or violations of the Company’s Code of Conduct. Complaints can be escalated to the Chief Ethics Counsellor or the Chairman of the Audit Committee for impartial review and resolution. Employees may also raise any ethical concerns through ethics@nelco.in or report issues to identified ethics committee members.
ii. Prevention of Sexual Harassment (POSH): Nelco has implemented a POSH Policy in compliance with statutory requirements. An Internal Committee (IC) is constituted to investigate and address complaints of sexual harassment, discrimination, or hostile behavior, ensuring confidentiality and fair proceedings.
iii. Health & Safety Grievance Portal: Employees and stakeholders have access to a dedicated portal to report health and safety-related grievances or observations. These submissions are reviewed by the Apex Safety Committee, and corrective actions are implemented promptly. Feedback on resolution is communicated to the complainant to ensure transparency.
These mechanisms collectively reinforce Nelco’s commitment to ethical conduct, workplace safety, and employee well-being.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOccupationalHealthAnd: This OHS management system is applicable to all our stakeholders involved in our business operations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DesclosureOfTheProcessesUsedTo: Nelco has an online portal for reporting safety related hazards, incidents, and observations. We have work permit system and SOPs which are strictly adhered to. We also conduct a HIRA exercise, wherein work process related Hazards are identified, risks assessed and mitigation plans monitored. An Apex safety committee along with working committees are constituted to regularly monitor safety needs. The committees conduct safety walks in respective areas to identify and mitigate any safety related observations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProcessesForWorkersToReportThe: Not applicable as there are no workers
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMeasuresTakenByTheE:
Nelco is committed to providing a safe, healthy, and secure workplace for all employees, contractors, and stakeholders. Occupational health and safety are integral to our operational philosophy, and we have instituted a comprehensive framework to proactively manage workplace risks and promote employee well-being. i. Leadership Oversight and Accountability: A robust governance structure underpins our safety management approach. This includes a central Apex Safety Committee, regional Safety Single Points of Contact (SPOCs), and trained Emergency Response Team (ERT) members across locations. Senior leadership, including the Managing Director & CEO, demonstrates visible commitment through regular safety walks, reviews, and evaluations. Safety performance indicators are integrated into performance management systems. ii. Hazard Identification and Risk Management: Nelco follows a structured Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment (HIRA) process to systematically identify, assess, and mitigate workplace hazards. Identified risks are classified into High, Medium, and Low categories. Appropriate control measures implemented through defined Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs). Periodic third-party safety assessments further strengthen our risk mitigation efforts and ensure continuous improvement. iii. Regulatory Compliance and Assurance Mechanisms: To ensure full compliance with applicable occupational health and safety regulations, Nelco leverages the Lateral Praxis System-that monitors statutory obligations and timely closures. We conduct regular internal audits and engage external agencies for independent assessments, enabling adherence to legal requirements and best practices in health and safety management. iv. Design and Operational Control: Safety is integrated into every aspect of our operations. We have detailed SOPs for each activity, which are regularly updated based on recommendations. Employees receive regular training to ensure they have the skills and knowledge to perform their jobs safely. Environmental monitoring, including air quality checks, helps maintain a safe working environment. v. Building Competency and Encouraging Safe Behaviors: We offer various training programs to enhance our employees’ safety knowledge and skills. Exceptional safety behaviors are recognized through our Suraksha awards, fostering a culture of safety. Continuous einforcement of safety norms through communication and training ensures that safety is always top of mind. vi. Communication and Empowerment: We use multiple communication channels, including town halls, emailers, posters, and safety induction programs, to keep everyone informed about safety measures. National Safety Week initiatives and third-party training sessions encourage active participation. Employees are empowered to report unsafe conditions and stop work if necessary. vii. Periodic Safety Audits: Electrical safety audits are conducted regularly by external experts at key offices in Mumbai and Delhi to enhance workplace safety standards. viii. Fire Safety Systems: A fire hydrant system has been installed at our Mahape office, which is regularly maintained to ensure operational readiness. A fire alarm & detection system is also operational in the facility. ix. Medical facilities: On-site medical provisions, including access to a doctor and health check-ups for employees underscore the company’s commitment to employee well-being. x. Hygienic Facilities: The provision of hygienic canteen facilities and office spaces further reinforces a conducive work environment. xi. Training Sessions: Regular training sessions on safety, ethics, and health are conducted to instil a culture of awareness and responsibility among the workforce. xii. Safety induction during onboarding: As part of the onboarding process, each new employee undergoes a safety briefing. The office conducts regular fire drills and provides training on fire equipment usage. Before being fully integrated into the system, all employees must complete the safety induction procedure. The company maintains a structured safety training schedule to foster a safety-conscious culture among its employees.
These measures collectively ensure that Nelco maintains a safe and healthy workplace, prioritizing the well-being of its employees.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: i. A dedicated portal is provided for employees and stakeholders to report any safety related observation, near miss or incident. All reports are evaluated by the Apex Safety committee and mitigation measures implemented are conveyed to the person reporting it. ii. Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment (HIRA): Developed and implemented HIRA to systematically identify workplace hazards and unsafe conditions, ensuring appropriate control measures are in place to mitigate risks. iii. Digitization of Permit-to-Work (PTW) System: Transitioned from a manual PTW system to a digital platform to enhance tracking, ensure compliance, and streamline approval processes through an online workflow. iv. Enhanced Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) Register: Established a comprehensive LOTO register to effectively monitor isolation activities, following a detailed assessment by a third party and TBEXG to address identified improvement areas. v. The HVAC assessment has identified opportunities to reduce energy consumption and optimize the cooling. Identified AC units which need replacement with ones having better performance and energy savings. vi. As a result of the Lightning risk assessment, a small part of the building is not adequately protected by the installed lightning arrestors. These are now being implemented to reduce risk to employees and property.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple3ExplanatoryText: Nelco allows its employees to continue their company-purchased medical coverage at their own cost
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksforComplaintsExplanator: Employees sensitized to report minor safety & health observations
Employees sensitized to report minor safety & health observations
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMeasuresUndertakenByT: During the invoicing process, the company verifies the compliance documents of its partners to confirm the payment of statutory dues. The vendor agreement stipulates the examination of vendors’ statutory dues, granting Nelco the right to conduct audits. Nelco ensures vendors fulfill their statutory obligations, including ESIC, PF, and Professional tax payments, to the appropriate authorities punctually. Cross-checking with employees of the vendors is conducted to verify timely salary payments, and compliance documents are reviewed. The agreement incorporates statutory compliance requirements, with emphasis on timely filing.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: i. Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment (HIRA): Developed and implemented HIRA to systematically identify workplace hazards and unsafe conditions, ensuring appropriate control measures are in place to mitigate risks. ii. Digitization of Permit-to-Work (PTW) System: Implemented a digital platform to enhance tracking, ensure compliance, and streamline approval processes through an online workflow. Any violation of safety conditions by the vendor results in a warning followed by disciplinary measures being implemented. iii. Enhanced Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) Register: Established a comprehensive LOTO register to effectively monitor isolation activities, following a detailed assessment by a third party and TBEXG to address identified improvement areas.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesForIdentif:
Nelco Limited employs a systematic approach to identify and engage with its key stakeholder groups. This process is integral to ensure that the company addresses the needs and expectations of all relevant parties. The key steps in this process include: i. Stakeholder Mapping: Nelco conducts comprehensive stakeholder mapping to identify all potential stakeholders, including employees, customers, suppliers, investors, regulatory bodies, and the community. This mapping is regularly updated to reflect any changes in the stakeholder landscape. ii. Engagement Channels: Various engagement channels are established to facilitate two-way communication with stakeholders. These channels include surveys, focus groups, meetings, and digital platforms, ensuring that stakeholders have multiple avenues to express their views and concerns. iii. Needs Assessment: Nelco performs regular needs assessments to understand the priorities and expectations of different stakeholder groups. This involves gathering feedback through structured interactions and analyzing the data to identify key themes and areas of interest. Materiality assessment conducted by external assessors help in better understanding of the priorities. iv. Prioritization: Based on the needs assessment, stakeholders are prioritized according to their influence and impact on the company’s operations. This helps Nelco allocate resources effectively and address the most critical stakeholder concerns. v. Continuous Monitoring: The company continuously monitors stakeholder interactions and feedback to ensure that its engagement strategies remain relevant and effective. This ongoing process allows Nelco to adapt to changing stakeholder needs and maintain strong relationships. vi. Reporting and Transparency: Nelco is committed to transparency and regularly reports on its stakeholder engagement activities. This includes sharing updates on how stakeholder feedback has been incorporated into business decisions and strategies.
By following these processes, Nelco Limited ensures that it remains responsive to the needs of its key stakeholders, fostering trust and collaboration.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProvideTheProcessesForConsulta: The company’s management consistently engages essential stakeholders, such as investors, customers, suppliers, and employees, through various channels and at different intervals. These interactions aim to grasp their requirements and gather suggestions to enhance both financial and non-financial performance.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesAsToHowTheIn: The Materiality assessment revised in the fiscal year 2023-24 involved in-depth discussions with specific stakeholder groups to identify and prioritize the material topics to align with the overall organizational goal of enhancing its sustainable growth. This aims to pinpoint environmental, social, and governance matters crucial to the company’s corporate sustainability and enabled Nelco to highlight and rank the topics, thereby facilitating the development of an ESG strategy aligned with Nelco’s business objectives and risks.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesOfEngagement: There are no concerns raised by identified vulnerable/marginalized stakeholder groups.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: E-mail, Meetings, Company website, Newspaper, Advertisements
Employee Portal, E-mail, Company website, MD open house, Notice Boards
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Training and awareness, Health and safety, Engagement activities, Operational Efficiency
Timely delivery and payments, Safety concerns, Compliances, Collaborations
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheInternalMechanismsI:
Nelco is committed to upholding human rights and has established comprehensive policies to support, respect, and safeguard the human rights of both its direct and indirect employees, including those of its subsidiaries. The internal mechanisms in place to redress grievances related to human rights issues include: i. Whistle-blower & Vigil Mechanism Policy: Nelco has adopted and implemented a Whistle-blower & Vigil Mechanism Policy to provide a system for employees to report concerns about unethical behavior, actual or suspected fraud, or violations of the Company’s Code of Conduct or ethics policy. This mechanism ensures that employees can report their concerns to management without fear of retaliation. ii. POSH Committee: Nelco has a dedicated POSH (Prevention of Sexual Harassment) Committee to handle complaints related to sexual harassment. This committee extends its coverage to associates, franchisee engineers, and vendors, ensuring a safe and respectful workplace for all. iii. Tata Code of Conduct (TCoC) Review Mechanism: Concerns regarding violations of the Tata Code of Conduct (TCoC) are addressed internally through a review mechanism managed by the Ethics Counsellor and Senior Management. This process ensures that any violations of the TCoC are thoroughly investigated, and appropriate actions are taken. iv. Supplier Code of Conduct (SCoC): Suppliers are required to adhere to norms relating to human rights including Non-discrimination, Anti-harassment, Child and forced labor, Wages and benefits, Freedom of association and collective bargaining, Environment, health & safety.
These mechanisms collectively ensure that Nelco Limited maintains a workplace environment that respects and protects human rights, providing employees with the necessary channels to voice their concerns and seek redressal.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsToPreventAdverseCons: In alignment with the Whistleblower Policy and POSH Policy, the Company includes a section dedicated to safeguarding the anonymity of the complainant. All instances of discrimination and harassment are handled in strict accordance with Company’ policy. Complaints relating to Whistleblower can be directly made to the Chairman of the Audit Committee hence protecting the complainant against adverse consequences.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT: Not applicable, there were no issues reported to take any corrective actions.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfABusinessProcessBeing: Although there were no grievances or complaints registered during the year, we have stepped up awareness campaigns regarding cybercrimes and crimes against women. We have also taken steps to ensure that our value chain partners are aware of our policies and specifically list them in purchase orders released to vendors.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheScopeAndCoverageOf:
The Company undertakes Human Rights Due Diligence (HRDD) as an integral part of its governance and risk management framework, with a focus on promoting fair employment practices, ethical conduct, and compliance with applicable labor laws. The scope and coverage of human rights due diligence during the reporting period included the following:
i. Employee Satisfaction Surveys: Periodic employee satisfaction surveys were conducted to assess employee well-being, workplace safety, inclusiveness, equal opportunity, fair treatment, and grievance redressal mechanisms. The survey outcomes were analyzed by management to identify potential human rights risks and areas for improvement, and suitable action plans were initiated wherever required.
ii. Leadership & Business Ethics (LBE) Survey: A dedicated survey on Leadership & Business Ethics was administered to evaluate employee perceptions regarding ethical conduct, transparency, non-discrimination, prevention of harassment, and leadership accountability. The findings were reviewed by senior management and used to reinforce ethical standards and strengthen internal controls.
iii. Wages and Labour Practices Verification: Compliance relating to timely payment of wages, statutory dues, and adherence to applicable labour laws was subject to regular verification through Internal & external audits. These audits did not report any material non-compliance related to payment of wages or employee entitlements during the reporting period.
iv. Governance and Oversight: The outcomes of the surveys and audit findings formed part of management reviews and were used to strengthen policies, controls, and training programs relating to human rights, ethics, and employee welfare.
Overall, the Company’s human rights due diligence processes are designed to identify, prevent, mitigate, and address potential human rights impacts within its operations and workforce, and are reviewed periodically to ensure continued effectiveness.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT: Not applicable as no major issues were reported upon which the corrective actions might be required. However, as a process the Company ensures training of new joiners and existing employees on such requirements. No significant risk / concerns raised. However, external assessment by labor consultant, awareness on ethics and reporting measures are continued efforts to educate the employees and all stakeholders (EHS).
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple5ExplanatoryText:
For details of remuneration of the Board Members, kindly refer to the Corporate Governance Report which forms a part of this Annual Report
Note: i. Managing Directors & Chief Executive Officer is considered in BOD ii. Key Managerial Personnel includes, CFO and Company Secretary
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AMechanismForZeroLiquidDischar: Since Nelco is service based company, mechanism of Zero Liquid discharge is not applicable.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfProjectRelatedToReduc:
As Nelco Limited embarks on its ESG journey, it is committed to reducing greenhouse gas emissions through forward thinking initiatives. These initiatives include: i. Upgrading Systems: Replacing outdated systems with energy-efficient alternatives to reduce overall energy consumption and emissions. ii. Energy Audits: Conducting regular energy audits to identify areas for improvement and implement energy saving measures. iii. Emission Control Devices: Deploy emission control devices for generators to minimize emissions during operations. iv. Renewable Energy Integration: Explore the feasibility of integrating renewable energy sources into our operations to further minimize environmental impact.
Through these efforts, Nelco Limited aims to significantly reduce its greenhouse gas emissions and contribute to a more sustainable future.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfWasteManagementPracti: All generated E-Waste, are disposed of by a certified e-waste vendor authorized by the Maharashtra Pollution Control Board (MPCB). The company emphasizes the use of RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances) compliant products.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TypeOfOperations: Monitoring and management of remote VSATs
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TheEntityHasNotApplicableEnvir: Nelco operates as a service-based company, and its operations do not emit any harmful substances. However, the organization prioritizes environmental compliance with all rules and regulations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSignificantDirectAndI: There is no impact of the entity on biodiversity as it is only a satellite teleport situated in this area.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DisclosureWebLinkOfEntityAtWhi:
Nelco has established a Business Continuity Plan (BCP) policy and procedures aligned with the ISMS framework for IT applications and infrastructure. This policy ensures the continuity of critical business processes and recovery in the event of significant disruptions. The BCP addresses all significant business processes and emphasize on: i. Identification of risks ii. Identification of business requirements for continuity iii. Quantification of impact of potential threat iv. Establishing recovery priorities by defining Recovery Time
The plan is developed in accordance with operational requirements and identifies disaster types along with typical recovery and management methods.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DiscloseAnySignificantAdverseI: Nelco is now monitoring Scope 3 emissions which includes emission resulting from key value chain parameters.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple6ExplanatoryText: i. Nelco has started a system to monitor and disclose scope 3 emissions. ii. The company is not eligible for registration with MoEFCC to procure green credits.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: i. Digital initiatives for monitoring emissions & energy consumption. ii. Conducted energy audits to identify areas for improvement.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: i. Improved visibility of ESG trends enables quicker actions towards the ESG goals. ii. Reduction in emissions and energy consumption.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:CorrectiveActionTakenForInitia: Monitoring initiated. Corrective actions will be considered after observing trend for few years.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: i. Line up actions aimed at improving resource efficiency across our operations. ii. Established Key Performance Indicators to monitor and achieve our ESG goals.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: i. Establish accountability of leadership and teams towards achieving ESG goals.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheArea: Our Operations are not located in water stressed areas
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PublicPolicyAdvocated: Nelco participated in several industry groupings and associations including DOT, TRAI, BIF, ISPA etc.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MethodResortedForSuchAdvocacy: Nelco has advocated for policies related to satellite communication services, data communication services, digital transformation like satcom spectrum management & pricing, licensing framework. Nelco’s membership at these regulatory associations serves as a medium for such advocacy.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsToReceive: Nelco has developed a mechanism to receive and redress grievances of the community and local communities can voice their concerns by reaching out through the email addresses provided on the website: ethics@nelco.in
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:CSRProject: Jan Vikas Society Mermier Bal Ashram
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AspirationalDistrictOfCSRProje: Turbhe- e-Vidya, Nelco sponsored Community Learning Centre
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsInPlaceTo:
Customer satisfaction is a key focus for Nelco Limited. We maintain a robust system for monitoring consumer complaints and feedback. Our complaint management process includes registering complaints, conducting root cause analysis by the relevant department, engaging with customers through various stakeholders, and resolving issues with customer feedback.
Complaints can be received through: i. Service Desk portal ii. Email (helpdesk@nelco.in) iii. Help Desk phone number: 022-67918728
We have defined SLAs with every customer for responding to and resolution of their complaints. Additionally, customers can address any ethics-related complaints to our dedicated email id: ethics@nelco.in. A portal is also provided for customers to report any safety-related issues.
We also have a process of obtaining customer feedback on a regular basis and through annual customer satisfaction surveys conducted by independent third party agency.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT:
Nelco is dedicated to safeguarding sensitive information, which contributes to fostering trust and confidence in its products and services. The company implements and upholds efficient controls to oversee the confidentiality, integrity, and accessibility of its information assets.
i. Policy Nelco has Board approved policies concerning information security, cybersecurity, and data privacy. While the Data Privacy policy is accessible to both internal and external parties, the Information Security and Cybersecurity policies are available exclusively on the company’s intranet. Web-link: https://www.nelco.in/privacy-policy.php#
ii. Certifications Nelco holds ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certification, which is an internationally recognized standard for Information Security Management Systems (ISMS).
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WeblinkWhereInformationOnProdu: Details about the Company’s goods and services can be accessed on its website https://www.nelco.in/key-services/vsat.php Given that Nelco operates in the B2B sector, much of its customer engagement occurs through individual interactions, where consultative selling is often employed, considering the unique requirements of each customer.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StepsTakenToInformAndEducateCo: Nelco’s solutions are tailored for Business to Business (B2B) use, focusing on delivering satellite-based communication services. The company has established guidelines for clients to adhere to while on-site, specifically addressing consumer safety. These guidelines are communicated to customers via email during the contracting phase, and Nelco also provides safety information and best practices through presentations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsInPlaceToInformConsu: In case there is a planned downtime of service, customers are informed in advance. All the approvals for the same are taken prior to the disruption
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfProductInformationOnT: Consumers are provided with product information that exceeds the requirements mandated by local laws. Since, Nelco offers services using technical products, displaying product information directly on the product is not feasible. However, these details are communicated to customers during the pre-sale process.
